idea: What if humans tasted food with their hands?
selection_reason: This idea is incredibly simple, visceral, and universally understood by any human of any age. It instantly triggers funny and relatable daily scenarios—like the horror of shaking hands, petting a dog, or accidentally touching garbage—making it perfect for a highly engaging, fast-paced video.

dialogs.text: What if your hands had tastebuds—and touching food was how you tasted it?
dialogs.flux_prompt: Pixar feature animation style, Disney 3D render, hyper-expressive characters, cinematic studio lighting, vibrant saturated color palette, soft volumetric rim light, shallow depth of field, 8K render quality, no text, no watermark, character-focused composition, exaggerated expressive faces, warm cinematic color grade, photorealistic textures on stylized geometry — a young man looking in absolute shock and awe at his own fingertips, which have tiny, cute, stylized glowing tastebuds on them

dialogs.text: Eating a burger would mean plunging your bare hands right into the greasy, delicious cheese.

dialogs.text: High-fives would become extremely intimate—and potentially taste like whatever they had for lunch.

dialogs.text: Handshakes? Completely banned. No one wants to taste a stranger's sweaty subway commute.

dialogs.text: Grocery shopping would be a sensory overload—just touching an onion could make you cry.
